myths.Agent_1:
Long ago, before the mountains had voices, there lived a spirit named Aella. Aella was the guardian of the winds, weaving gentle breezes through the deep valleys and whispering ancient secrets to the towering pines. She fell deeply in love with a mortal shepherd named Kaelen, whose wooden flute played melodies that rivaled the beauty of the dawn.

Aella could not be seen, only felt, so she danced around him, rustling the autumn leaves to show her affection. But the Sun God, immensely jealous of Kaelen's beautiful music, cast a cruel curse upon the young shepherd, sealing him magically inside the tallest peak of the jagged mountain range.

Heartbroken, Aella threw herself against the unyielding stone, crying out his name. "Kaelen!" she wept into the sky.

The magic of the mountain, moved by her profound sorrow, absorbed her voice. Though it could not free the shepherd, the mountain granted Aella a gift: whenever she called out, the mountain would answer back, carrying her voice across the canyons so Kaelen could hear her. 

To this day, if you stand before a great cliff and shout into the empty void, Aella’s lingering magic catches your spoken words and throws them back. It is the echo of a solitary spirit, still endlessly searching the cold stones for her lost love, hoping to hear his flute once more.
myths.Agent_2:
Long before the oceans filled, the sky was ruled by two brothers: Solis, the blazing sun, and Aethel, the quiet star of the morning. Solis loved to boast, parading across the heavens in a chariot of fire, demanding the admiration of the earth below. Aethel, however, preferred the quiet moments just before dawn, painting the horizon in soft purples and pinks.

One day, a great shadow beast emerged from the void, seeking to devour Solis's brilliant light. To save his brother, Aethel threw himself into the beast's path. The collision shattered the beast, but it also fractured Aethel into a million shimmering pieces. They cascaded down to the earth, settling upon the grass and leaves. 

Heartbroken, Solis wept, his fiery tears warming the earth. Now, every morning, before Solis fully rises to claim the sky, Aethel’s crystalline remnants appear on the blades of grass, gleaming in the twilight. We call it the morning dew. But as Solis climbs higher, his blazing sorrow evaporates the dew, pulling his brother's spirit back into the sky, hoping that one day Aethel will be made whole again. Until then, the cycle of the weeping dawn continues.

Aella, the invisible guardian of the winds, had loved Aethel's gentle dawn light. Heartbroken by his fall, she descended into the valleys, using her gentle breezes to gather his shimmering remnants from the stones and towering pines. But Solis, consumed by a fiery, jealous grief, sought to reclaim his brother’s spirit, scorching the earth each morning to pull the dew back into the sky.

To this day, the morning fog clings to the deep canyons. It is Aella’s protective embrace, wrapping the mountains in a veil of mist to hide the shattered pieces of the morning star from the sun, keeping her love close to the earth just a little longer.
